class.name: FmodAudioEffectPhaser
class.brief_description: Phaser effect using flanger DSP for sweeping filter effects.
class.description: FmodAudioEffectPhaser creates a phaser effect by using FMOD's flange DSP. It produces a sweeping filter effect by modulating the frequency range of all-pass filters. Note: While phasers and flangers have different underlying principles, this implementation uses flanger DSP to approximate phaser-like effects. Use this effect to add movement, space, and character to audio.

method.name: get_depth
method.description: Returns the filter frequency sweep depth.

method.name: get_range_max_hz
method.description: Returns the maximum frequency of the LFO sweep in Hertz.

method.name: get_range_min_hz
method.description: Returns the minimum frequency of the LFO sweep in Hertz.

method.name: get_rate_hz
method.description: Returns the modulation rate in Hertz.

method.name: set_depth
method.description: Sets the filter frequency sweep depth. Range: 0.1 to 4.0. Higher values create more pronounced filtering effects.

method.name: set_feedback
method.description: Sets the feedback amount. Range: 0.1 to 0.9. Higher feedback creates more resonant, intense phasing effects.

method.name: set_range_max_hz
method.description: Sets the maximum frequency of the LFO sweep in Hertz. Range: 10 to 10000.

method.name: set_range_min_hz
method.description: Sets the minimum frequency of the LFO sweep in Hertz. Range: 10 to 10000.

method.name: set_rate_hz
method.description: Sets the modulation rate in Hertz. Range: 0.5 to 20.0. Higher rates create faster sweeping effects.

members.member: The filter frequency sweep depth. Range: 0.1 to 4.0. Higher values create more pronounced filtering effects.
The feedback amount. Range: 0.1 to 0.9. Higher feedback creates more resonant, intense phasing effects.
The maximum frequency of the LFO sweep in Hz. Range: 10 to 10000.
The minimum frequency of the LFO sweep in Hz. Range: 10 to 10000.
The modulation rate in Hz. Range: 0.5 to 20.0. Higher rates create faster sweeping effects.
